program.name: Head Start
program.description:
Free preschool and family support for children ages 3 to 5 in Kansas. Head Start provides early learning, health care, healthy meals, and family support services at no cost to families.

Services are provided in-person at local Head Start centers. Your child will attend preschool classes and receive meals, health screenings, and developmental support directly at the program site.

Families with low income can apply. Children who receive SNAP (food stamps), TANF (cash help), or SSI (disability benefits) qualify automatically. Children in foster care also qualify.

Head Start is run by local agencies across Kansas. Services and availability vary by location. There may be a waitlist in some areas.

To apply, find your local Head Start program using the link below. They will help you check eligibility. They will also help you complete the application and gather any required documents.
program.description_short: Free preschool for children ages 3 to 5
program.apply_button_description: Find your local Head Start agency
program.website_description: Free preschool, meals, and health services for children ages 3 to 5 from low-income families

documents.text: Child's birth certificate or proof of age
Proof of income (pay stubs, tax returns, benefit award letters)
Proof of home address (lease, utility bill, etc.)
Proof of SNAP, TANF, or SSI benefits (if applicable)
